data.title: HSBC Confirms Over 17.1 Billion Voting Rights in Latest Capital Update
data.description:
End of Quarter Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock powerful investing tools and data-driven insights with TipRanks Premium for more confident investment decisions.
  • Discover top stock picks and new investment opportunities through TipRanks' Smart Investor Newsletter.

HSBC Holdings ( (GB:HSBA) ) has issued an update.

HSBC Holdings plc has reported that, as of 29 June 2026, its issued share capital stands at 17,183,563,842 ordinary shares of US$0.50 each, with no shares held in treasury, resulting in an equivalent total number of voting rights. This disclosure helps shareholders and regulators calculate ownership thresholds and assess reporting obligations under UK and Hong Kong transparency regimes, reinforcing HSBC’s compliance and providing clarity for institutional and retail investors.

The update underscores HSBC’s ongoing adherence to disclosure requirements across its multiple listings and offers a clear denominator for investors tracking changes in their shareholdings or filing substantial shareholding notices. By standardising this information, the bank supports orderly market functioning and enhances transparency in its global capital markets presence.

More about HSBC Holdings

HSBC Holdings plc is a global banking and financial services group headquartered in London, providing retail, commercial, and investment banking as well as wealth management across key markets in Europe, Asia, the Americas, and the Middle East. The group’s shares are listed on multiple stock exchanges, including London and Hong Kong, reflecting its broad international investor base.

data.title: Dynagreen Leaves Nearly Entire RMB2.36 Billion Convertible Bond Issue Unconverted
data.description:
End of Quarter Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock powerful investing tools and data-driven insights with TipRanks Premium for more confident investment decisions.
  • Discover top stock picks and new investment opportunities through TipRanks' Smart Investor Newsletter.

Dynagreen Environmental Protection Group Co., Ltd. Class H ( (HK:1330) ) has shared an update.

Dynagreen Environmental Protection Group reported that its A share convertible corporate bonds, issued in February 2022 for RMB2.36 billion with a six-year term and listed in Shanghai under the name Dynagreen Convertible Bonds, have undergone multiple conversion price adjustments following consecutive annual and interim equity distributions and an A-share incentive scheme. These price resets, which lowered the conversion price from RMB9.82 at issuance to RMB8.67 per share as of July 2026, are designed to reflect shareholder returns and employee incentives while maintaining bondholder conversion economics.

In the second quarter of 2026, only 112 A shares were converted from the Dynagreen Convertible Bonds, worth RMB1,000, bringing total converted bonds since 2022 to RMB143,000, or just 14,798 shares, representing 0.0011% of pre-conversion share capital. As of 30 June 2026, RMB2.359857 billion of the bonds, or 99.9939% of the original issue size, remained outstanding and available for conversion until February 2028, indicating limited bondholder appetite to convert so far and leaving Dynagreen with a largely intact convertible debt overhang on its balance sheet.

More about Dynagreen Environmental Protection Group Co., Ltd. Class H

Dynagreen Environmental Protection Group Co., Ltd. is a PRC-incorporated environmental services company focused on waste-to-energy and related environmental protection projects, with its A shares and convertible bonds traded in mainland China and H shares listed in Hong Kong. The group finances its operations partly through onshore A-share convertible corporate bonds issued on the Shanghai Stock Exchange, reflecting its reliance on capital markets to support long-term infrastructure and environmental initiatives.

Average Trading Volume: 1,751,659

Technical Sentiment Signal: Buy

Current Market Cap: HK$10.31B

data.title: Richly Field China Development Narrows Loss but Takes Heavy Impairments in 2026
data.description:
End of Quarter Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock powerful investing tools and data-driven insights with TipRanks Premium for more confident investment decisions.
  • Discover top stock picks and new investment opportunities through TipRanks' Smart Investor Newsletter.

Richly Field China Development Limited ( (HK:0313) ) has issued an announcement.

Richly Field China Development Limited reported a sharp decline in revenue to HK$41.9 million for the year ended 31 March 2026, down from HK$123.2 million a year earlier, with gross profit nearly halving amid continued weakness in its property operations. The group posted a net loss of HK$214.1 million, narrower than the prior year, but results were heavily hit by substantial impairments on goodwill, other receivables and investment properties, highlighting ongoing asset quality pressures and underlining the financial strain on the developer as it navigates a difficult real estate market.

The company also recorded lower selling and administrative expenses and a modest income tax credit, but financing costs remained high and profitability was further undermined by valuation losses on its investment properties. While some reversal of expected credit losses on trade receivables provided minor relief, the scale of impairments indicates continued headwinds for stakeholders, signalling persistent balance-sheet risks and limited near-term earnings visibility for the Hong Kong-listed developer.

More about Richly Field China Development Limited

Richly Field China Development Limited is a Hong Kong-listed property developer with operations conducted through its subsidiaries. The group focuses on investment properties and related real estate activities in mainland China, deriving revenue from property sales and associated services in a challenging market environment.

Average Trading Volume: 514,892

data.title: Suzhou Ribo Hits First Milestone in Madrigal siRNA Liver Disease Partnership
data.description:
End of Quarter Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock powerful investing tools and data-driven insights with TipRanks Premium for more confident investment decisions.
  • Discover top stock picks and new investment opportunities through TipRanks' Smart Investor Newsletter.

Suzhou Ribo Life Science Co., Ltd. Class H ( (HK:6938) ) has shared an update.

Suzhou Ribo Life Science has reached the first candidate drug nomination milestone in its siRNA collaboration with Madrigal Pharmaceuticals, advancing an RNA therapeutic for liver diseases with an initial focus on metabolic dysfunction-associated steatohepatitis. The achievement triggers the start of IND-enabling studies, positioning the partners to move toward clinical development in a large, underserved global MASH population, while the company cautions that there is no guarantee the collaboration will ultimately lead to completed transactions or approved products.

The milestone underscores Suzhou Ribo’s strategy to apply its validated RiboGalSTAR liver-targeting platform to high-burden liver indications where treatment needs remain significant. Successful progress in this program could strengthen the company’s standing in the RNA therapeutics field and potentially create long-term value for shareholders and patients if subsequent development stages are achieved.

More about Suzhou Ribo Life Science Co., Ltd. Class H

Suzhou Ribo Life Science Co., Ltd. is a China-based biopharmaceutical company specializing in RNA therapeutics, particularly small interfering RNA, with a focus on liver-targeted drug delivery via its proprietary RiboGalSTAR platform. The company develops innovative treatments for serious liver diseases and partners with global pharma players to advance its pipeline.

data.title: Insilico Strikes Up to USD600 Million AI Drug Discovery Pact With Takeda
data.description:
End of Quarter Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock powerful investing tools and data-driven insights with TipRanks Premium for more confident investment decisions.
  • Discover top stock picks and new investment opportunities through TipRanks' Smart Investor Newsletter.

An update from InSilico Medicine Cayman TopCo ( (HK:3696) ) is now available.

InSilico Medicine has entered a strategic collaboration with Japanese biopharmaceutical group Takeda to deploy its end-to-end AI drug discovery platform, Pharma.AI, to identify novel, clinically differentiated drug candidates across Takeda’s core therapeutic areas. Under the agreement, Insilico will lead AI-driven molecular discovery, while Takeda will take selected candidates through global clinical development, gaining exclusive worldwide rights to develop, manufacture, and commercialize any resulting therapeutics.

The deal provides Insilico with about USD60 million in upfront and near-term payments and could reach a total value of up to USD600 million through success-based milestones, plus tiered royalties on future product sales. The partnership underscores growing pharma demand for AI-enabled R&D, strengthens Insilico’s positioning as a key AI partner to large drugmakers, and signals potential long-term upside for the company’s revenue streams and shareholders if the collaboration yields commercially successful medicines.

More about InSilico Medicine Cayman TopCo

InSilico Medicine Cayman TopCo is a Hong Kong–listed global biotechnology company that integrates artificial intelligence and automation to accelerate drug discovery and innovation in life sciences. Through its proprietary Pharma.AI platform and in-house R&D capabilities, it develops drug candidates targeting unmet medical needs in areas such as fibrosis, oncology, immunology, pain, and obesity and metabolic disorders, while also extending its AI technologies to advanced materials, agriculture, nutritional products, and veterinary medicine.

data.title: Aceso Life Science Delays 2026 Results, Faces Trading Suspension in Hong Kong
data.description:
End of Quarter Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock powerful investing tools and data-driven insights with TipRanks Premium for more confident investment decisions.
  • Discover top stock picks and new investment opportunities through TipRanks' Smart Investor Newsletter.

Aceso Life Science Group Limited ( (HK:0474) ) has provided an announcement.

Aceso Life Science Group Limited has announced it will miss the 30 June 2026 deadline to publish its audited annual results for the year ended 31 March 2026, citing the need for additional time to collect valuation data for certain financial assets, associates and investment property required by its auditor. The board has decided not to issue unaudited management accounts to avoid confusing investors, and stresses that group operations remain normal while it works with the auditor to complete remaining procedures and release the results as soon as practicable.

Because of the delay in audited results, the company expects it may also miss the 31 July 2026 deadline to dispatch its 2026 annual report, which would put it in non-compliance with listing rules until a new date is announced. The board meeting originally scheduled for 30 June 2026 to approve the results has been postponed, and trading in the company’s shares on the Hong Kong Stock Exchange will be suspended from 2 July 2026 pending publication of the overdue annual results, a move that directly affects shareholder liquidity and market perception of the group’s governance and reporting reliability.

More about Aceso Life Science Group Limited

Aceso Life Science Group Limited is a Hong Kong-listed company engaged in the life sciences sector through a group structure incorporated in the Cayman Islands. The company operates across various investments and assets, including financial assets, associates and investment properties, reflecting a diversified portfolio within the broader healthcare and life science-related investment space.

data.title: Abbisko and AstraZeneca Launch Joint Trial of Novel NSCLC Combination
data.description:
End of Quarter Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock powerful investing tools and data-driven insights with TipRanks Premium for more confident investment decisions.
  • Discover top stock picks and new investment opportunities through TipRanks' Smart Investor Newsletter.

Abbisko Cayman Limited ( (HK:2256) ) has shared an announcement.

Abbisko Therapeutics has entered a strategic collaboration with AstraZeneca to conduct a multicenter Phase I/II clinical trial combining lumipodlin, a first‑in‑class oral PD‑L1 inhibitor, with AstraZeneca’s EGFR‑TKI Tagrisso in patients with EGFR‑mutated, PD‑L1 positive locally advanced or metastatic non‑small cell lung cancer. The investigational new drug application for this combination was cleared by China’s National Medical Products Administration, positioning Abbisko to lead the Phase II study and share trial responsibilities with AstraZeneca, potentially strengthening its clinical pipeline in a high‑need NSCLC segment while highlighting the promise of oral immune‑oncology agents alongside established targeted therapies.

More about Abbisko Cayman Limited

Abbisko Cayman Limited, through its subsidiary Abbisko Therapeutics, operates in the biopharmaceutical industry with a focus on innovative oncology therapies. The company is developing lumipodlin (ABSK043), a novel orally bioavailable small‑molecule PD‑L1 inhibitor aimed at treating non‑small cell lung cancer and addressing unmet needs in EGFR‑mutated, PD‑L1 positive NSCLC.

data.title: CALB Raises 2026 Cap on Connected Construction Deal Amid Capacity Expansion
data.description:

  • Discover top stock picks and new investment opportunities through TipRanks' Smart Investor Newsletter.

CALB Co., Ltd. Class H ( (HK:3931) ) has provided an update.

CALB Group has further amended its 2024–2026 Engineering and Construction Framework Agreement with Jiangsu Chengdong Construction, a connected party, by entering into a third supplemental agreement to raise the annual spending cap for 2026. The revision reflects higher-than-expected construction demand across the group’s industrial parks and facilities, indicating ongoing capacity build-out while keeping the core commercial terms of the framework unchanged.

Because Jiangsu Chengdong Construction is an associate of a substantial shareholder, the revised 2026 cap constitutes a continuing connected transaction under Hong Kong listing rules and triggers reporting, announcement and annual review requirements, but is exempt from circular and independent shareholder approval. Historical fees of over RMB380 million in 2024 and rising spending in 2025 and early 2026 underline the scale of CALB’s construction programme and the regulatory importance of monitoring related-party arrangements.

with a HK$36.00 price target.

see the HK:3931 Stock Forecast page.

data.title: SSY Group Wins NMPA Approvals for Methocarbamol Tablets and Phloroglucinol Bulk Drug
data.description:
End of Quarter Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock powerful investing tools and data-driven insights with TipRanks Premium for more confident investment decisions.
  • Discover top stock picks and new investment opportunities through TipRanks' Smart Investor Newsletter.

SSY Group ( (HK:2005) ) has shared an update.

SSY Group has become the first entity in mainland China to secure National Medical Products Administration approval for the production and registration of 0.5g Methocarbamol Tablets, a muscle relaxant used to treat joint and muscle sprains, lumbar muscle strain and sciatica. The drug’s consistency evaluation status underscores the group’s capability to meet stringent regulatory standards in the competitive generic medicines market.

The company also obtained NMPA registration approval for Phloroglucinol Dihydrate as a bulk drug, following its earlier clearance for Phloroglucinol Injection, enabling broader use in preparations targeting acute spasmodic pain in digestive, urinary and gynecological systems. These approvals expand SSY Group’s pain-management portfolio and signal continued progress in product development, which may enhance its positioning in China’s hospital and prescription drug segments.

with a HK$5.00 price target.

More about SSY Group

SSY Group Limited is a pharmaceutical company based in China, focusing on the development, production and registration of chemical drugs for the domestic healthcare market. The group’s portfolio includes muscle relaxants and treatments for pain related to digestive, urinary and gynecological conditions, reflecting a strategy centered on specialty therapeutics approved by Chinese regulators.

data.title: Venus Medtech Explores Up to RMB500 Million Investment via Shares and Convertible Bonds
data.description:
End of Quarter Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock powerful investing tools and data-driven insights with TipRanks Premium for more confident investment decisions.
  • Discover top stock picks and new investment opportunities through TipRanks' Smart Investor Newsletter.

Venus Medtech (Hangzhou), Inc. Class H ( (HK:2500) ) just unveiled an update.

Venus Medtech (Hangzhou) Inc. has signed a non-binding investment term sheet with Hangzhou Hi-Tech, Hangzhou Yingzhiqin and other potential investors for a prospective capital injection of up to RMB500 million. The funding would be raised through a mix of new share subscriptions and new convertible bonds, with pricing linked to the recent average trading price of the company’s Hong Kong-listed shares and capped at HK$2.5 per share.

Under the proposal, investors would subscribe for new shares at 100% of the 20-day average closing price and for new convertible bonds at 110% of that average, both subject to the price cap, potentially providing Venus Medtech with additional growth capital and balance sheet flexibility. The parties have agreed to pay RMB100 million in earnest money within seven business days, which is refundable if the transactions fail due to the company’s fault, but the deal remains subject to definitive documentation, regulatory approvals and shareholder mandate, meaning there is no assurance it will proceed and investors are urged to act cautiously.

More about Venus Medtech (Hangzhou), Inc. Class H

Venus Medtech (Hangzhou) Inc. is a China-based medical technology company focused on developing and commercializing cardiovascular implantable devices and related interventional solutions. Listed in Hong Kong, the company targets both domestic and international markets for advanced heart valve therapies and other structural heart disease treatments.
